Title: A retrotransposon insertion upstream of Arabidopsis thaliana CRK8 receptor-like kinase modulates a trade-off between pathogen defense and salt tolerance

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; natural accessions including Rub, Sha, Bla-1, Oy-0, Bor-1, Eds-1, Nok-3, Ws-0
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: alkaloid; reactive oxygen species; thiol antioxidant
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense and oxidative stress response; contributes to quantitative disease resistance
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cysteine-rich receptor-like kinase (CRK); DUF26/GnK2 ectodomain; transmembrane helix; serine/threonine kinase domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed at low basal level in healthy leaves, induced after S. sclerotiorum inoculation; constitutively higher in accessions carrying AT4TE56270
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in healthy and inoculated plants carrying AT4TE56270; induced in leaves after S. sclerotiorum infection; high expression associates with reduced germination under salt
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS in natural accessions; qRT-PCR; T-DNA mutant analysis (crk8.1, crk8.3); RNA-seq; lesion growth phenotyping; DAB staining for H2O2; camalexin LC-MS quantification; salt germination assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, ENA PRJEB88084; genome resequencing from 1001 Genomes/SALK datasets; metabolite profiling of camalexin derivatives
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sclerotinia sclerotiorum inoculation; 120 mM NaCl during germination
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes quantitative disease resistance to S. sclerotiorum, hydrogen peroxide accumulation, glutathione-associated defense responses, and camalexin accumulation; negatively regulates germination under salt stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural variation upstream of CRK8 is associated with quantitative disease resistance to S. sclerotiorum. 2. Higher CRK8 expression correlates with TE presence and lower disease severity. 3. CRK8 knockdown reduces lesion resistance, PR1/PR4 induction, H2O2 accumulation, and camalexin production. 4. Lower CRK8 expression improves germination on salt, revealing a defense–salt tolerance trade-off.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher CRK8 expression improves pathogen defense but reduces salt-tolerant germination; lower expression enhances salt germination but increases disease susceptibility
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune biotic–abiotic stress trade-off through promoter editing or allele selection depending on pathogen versus salinity pressure
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a receptor-like kinase regulating defense/salt responses rather than a pathway enzyme or canonical transcription factor. The upstream TE AT4TE56270 acts as cis-regulatory polymorphism affecting CRK8 expression.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: TE-associated regulation is hypothesized; discussion suggests possible effects via siRNA enrichment and DNA methylation around AT4TE56270, but no direct experimental validation in this paper
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CRK8 promoter with presence/absence polymorphism of upstream Copia retrotransposon AT4TE56270
